title: London, Glasgow, Inverness & Edinburgh.

trains.name: Trains & Taxi Transfers
trains.blurb: Scheduled inter-city trains linking London with Glasgow, Inverness and Edinburgh, with private transfers at each end.

transport.1: London airport to London hotel private transfer.
transport.2: London Hop-on Hop-off 1-day pass with a one-way Thames river cruise.
transport.4: Scheduled train from London to Glasgow, standard class (approx. 4h 30m).
transport.5: Scheduled train from Glasgow to Inverness, standard class (approx. 3h 15m), skirting Loch Lomond and the Cairngorms.
transport.7: Scheduled train from Inverness to Edinburgh, standard class (approx. 3h 30m).
transport.9: Edinburgh hotel to Edinburgh airport private transfer.

inclusions: London airport–hotel private transfer on arrival
London Hop-On Hop-Off 1-day ticket with one-way Thames river cruise
Train ticket London to Glasgow, standard class
Train ticket Glasgow to Inverness, standard class
Train ticket Inverness to Edinburgh, standard class
Edinburgh hotel–airport private transfer on departure

days.title: Arrive London
days.desc: Arrive into the British capital and transfer to your central hotel. Depending on arrival time, the rest of the day is free to explore nearby — Westminster Abbey, Big Ben and the Houses of Parliament are all within easy reach of most central hotels.
days.taste: A classic pub dinner of fish and chips is the perfect first-night introduction to London.

days.title: London sightseeing
days.desc: A full day discovering London with the Hop-on Hop-off ticket, taking in Buckingham Palace, the Tower of London and St Paul's Cathedral, before a one-way Thames river cruise gives a different perspective on the city's landmarks.
days.shopping: Oxford Street and Regent Street offer some of the world's best-known flagship department stores.
days.fallbackIncluded: London Hop-on Hop-off 1-day pass with one-way Thames river cruise.

days.title: London at leisure
days.desc: A free day to explore further — the British Museum, Covent Garden and the West End theatre district are all popular choices, or take a walk through Hyde Park and Kensington Gardens.
days.experience: Catch a West End show — London's theatre district rivals Broadway for range and quality.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional West End show or Harry Potter Studio Tour available.

days.title: London → Glasgow
days.desc: Travel north by train to Glasgow, Scotland's largest city. On arrival, explore George Square, the Merchant City and the Victorian architecture along Buchanan Street.
days.experience: Glasgow's Kelvingrove Art Gallery and Museum is one of Europe's great civic collections, and free to enter.

days.title: Glasgow → Inverness
days.desc: Continue by train into the Highlands, with the route skirting Loch Lomond and the Cairngorms before arriving in Inverness, capital of the Highlands, set on the River Ness.

days.title: Inverness & Loch Ness
days.desc: A day to explore the Highland capital and its surroundings — the ruins of 13th-century Urquhart Castle sit dramatically on the shores of Loch Ness, and boat cruises run regularly along the loch.
days.taste: Try a wee dram at one of the Highland whisky bars — Inverness is a gateway to the Speyside distilleries.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Loch Ness & Urquhart Castle tour available.

days.title: Inverness → Edinburgh
days.desc: Travel south by train through the Cairngorms and Perthshire to Edinburgh, Scotland's historic capital, arriving in time to explore the Old Town's cobbled closes and wynds.

days.title: Edinburgh sightseeing
days.desc: A full day in Edinburgh: Edinburgh Castle dominates the skyline atop its volcanic crag, the Royal Mile links it to the Palace of Holyroodhouse, and Arthur's Seat offers panoramic views over the city.
days.experience: Explore a Royal Mile whisky tasting experience, tracing Scotland's national drink through its regions.
days.fallbackIncluded: Day at leisure — optional Edinburgh Castle & Royal Mile walking tour available.

days.title: Depart Edinburgh
days.desc: Free time until your private transfer to Edinburgh airport, depending on flight time.

hotels.h3: Holiday Inn Express Glasgow City Centre Riverside
Holiday Inn Express Edinburgh City Centre

about.title: Best season
about.body: May to September offers the longest, mildest days for city and Highland sightseeing alike.

about.title: Weather
about.body: Temperate but changeable maritime climate, roughly 12–20°C in summer — pack a rain layer for any season.

about.title: Currency & docs
about.body: Pound Sterling (£) throughout; the UK is outside the Schengen area, so check entry requirements separately from EU travel.

goodToKnow.title: Tickets & timing
goodToKnow.body: Book Edinburgh Castle and Loch Ness cruises ahead in the summer months, when both can sell out days in advance.

goodToKnow.title: Rail travel
goodToKnow.body: Seat reservations are included on the London–Glasgow, Glasgow–Inverness and Inverness–Edinburgh trains; luggage space is generous but one large case per guest is recommended.

goodToKnow.title: What to pack
goodToKnow.body: Layers and a waterproof jacket in any month, plus comfortable shoes for cobbled streets in Edinburgh's Old Town.

goodToKnow.title: Money
goodToKnow.body: Cards are accepted almost everywhere across England and Scotland; tipping around 10-12.5% in restaurants is customary.

goodToKnow.title: Highlands distances
goodToKnow.body: Journeys between Highland towns can take longer than the map suggests — the scenery more than makes up for the extra time.

terms: All rates are net and per person for the package.
Child rates apply for children aged 2 to 11 years old. One child sharing a room with 2 adults, subject to hotel policy.
All rates are subject to availability at the time of booking. Europe Incoming will endeavour to match the rates quoted.
Rates are not applicable during trade fair periods, major European public holidays, and other major events.
City taxes are not included in the price.
If certain attractions are closed during specific periods, alternative options will be arranged.
All bookings must be confirmed at least 60 working days prior to arrival.
100% pre-payment required by bank transfer or credit card (Visa or Mastercard). All bank transfer charges covered by the Agent.
Vouchers will be issued after receipt of full payment.
